story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has issued multiple amendment directions in 2026, including Third Amendment Directions for Local Area Banks regarding financial statements, presentation, and disclosures, as well as Second Amendment Directions for Commercial Banks concerning the classification, valuation, and operation of investment portfolios. Separate Amendment Directions have also been introduced for Payments Banks and Local Area Banks, both addressing the classification, valuation, and operation of investment portfolios. These directives collectively aim to update regulatory frameworks for financial reporting and investment management across different banking categories.

story_clusters.summary: Piyush Goyal has urged industry leaders to leverage free trade agreements (FTAs) and emerging technologies amid global uncertainty, calling it an opportunity for faster reforms. He also emphasized the need for industries to reduce dependence on capital goods imports.

articles.title: Telangana HC Reiterates Mandatory GST Pre-Deposit for Appeals Despite Prior Tax Payment
articles.summary: Telangana High Court held that payment of outstanding GST dues during adjudication does not exempt a taxpayer from mandatory pre-deposit for filing an appeal. The Court clarified that penalty disputes must be decided by the appellate authority on merits. The post Telangana HC Reiterates Mandatory GST Pre-Deposit for Appeals Despite Prior Tax Payment appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Procedure for Shifting Registered Office Outside Local Limits Within Same State (Under Different ROC Jurisdiction)
articles.summary: This guide explains the legal steps a company must follow to shift its registered office within the same state but under a different Registrar of Companies (ROC) jurisdiction. It covers board approvals, Regional Director (RD) approval, statutory filings, public notices, and compliance timelines. The process ensures adherence to the Companies Act, 2013, and is relevant for businesses operating in India.

articles.title: RBI (Commercial Banks – Classification, Valuation, and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Second Amendment Directions, 2026
articles.summary: RBI has abolished the mandatory Investment Fluctuation Reserve requirement for commercial banks following changes in market risk and investment regulations. articles.title: RBI cancels the licence of The Yashwant Co-operative Bank Ltd., Phaltan
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), vide order dated May 18, 2026, has cancelled the licence of  The Yashwant Co-operative Bank Ltd., Phaltan , under Section 22 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). Consequently, the bank ceases to carry on banking business, with effect from the close of business on May 19, 2026. articles.title: Who is a ‘Secured Creditor’ Under IBC? Not the VAT Authority, Says Madras HC; Quashes Attachment of Property
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that VAT authorities are not 'secured creditors' under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The court quashed the attachment of a corporate debtor's property after liquidation proceedings began, stating that asset distribution must follow the IBC's waterfall mechanism (Section 53) and that Section 238 IBC overrides other laws. This decision prioritizes the IBC's insolvency resolution process over tax authorities' claims.
articles.raw_summary: Once an order of liquidation is passed and a statutory liquidator is appointed, the assets of the corporate debtor are required to be dealt with in accordance with Section 53 IBC i.e. the  waterfall mechanism  and the dues are to be paid in the order of priority set out in the section. Section 238 IBC contains a non-obstante clause giving primacy to the mechanism for distribution of the assets of the corporate debtor in the manner prescribed under the IBC.  The post Who

This initiative aims to streamline regulatory adherence through technology. The article also briefly mentions UpGrad's $1.7 billion valuation, which is unrelated to policy.
articles.raw_summary: Happy Wednesday! The government plans to translate legal provisions into software code that enforces compliance by design. This and more in today's ETtech Morning Dispatch.

articles.title: Coded compliance: Centre is eyeing 'law-to-code' to AI-proof data law
articles.summary: The Centre is exploring the 'law-to-code' approach, which involves converting legal rules into machine-executable algorithms (like Python code) to automate compliance and enforcement. This move aims to future-proof India's data laws against AI-related challenges. The initiative is being considered by the government to enhance regulatory efficiency and adapt to technological advancements.
articles.raw_summary: Law-to-code is the practice of translating legal rules into machine-executable algorithms such as a Python code that software can automatically process and enforce without human intervention.

articles.title: ‘No place for personal enmity in politics’, says G. Sudhakaran on swearing in former Kerala CM Pinarayi as MLA
articles.summary: G. Sudhakaran, newly sworn-in Pro Tem Speaker of the Kerala Assembly, stated that he sees no personal bias in administering the oath of office to former Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan as an MLA. The event is a routine procedural step in the assembly's functioning. Sudhakaran emphasized that his role is neutral and does not differentiate between MLAs based on past political roles.
articles.raw_summary: Sudhakaran, who has been sworn in as Pro Tem Speaker of Kerala Assembly, says he sees ‘no difference’ between Pinarayi Vijayan and any other MLA to whom he will be administering the oath of office on Thursday

articles.title: Haryana cabinet approves policy amendments for regularisation of unauthorised industrial units
articles.summary: The Haryana cabinet approved amendments to existing policies to regularize unauthorized industrial units across the state. The move aims to ensure basic civic amenities and infrastructure are provided in these areas. This decision impacts industrial regulation and urban development in Haryana.
articles.raw_summary: The Haryana cabinet here on Monday approved policy amendments to facilitate regularisation of unauthorised industrial establishments and ensure provision of basic civic amenities and infrastructure in such areas across the state.

articles.title: How Karunanidhi successfully headed a minority government for five years
articles.summary: The article examines how M. Karunanidhi successfully led a minority government in Tamil Nadu for five years, marking the last instance of a coalition government in the state since 1952. It highlights the rarity of such coalitions among Dravidian parties and their historical avoidance of the idea. The piece underscores the political challenges and strategies involved in sustaining a minority government.
articles.raw_summary: The last time Tamil Nadu witnessed a coalition government was in 1952; however, leaders of the Dravidian parties largely steered clear of the idea thereafter

articles.title: CIT(A) Cannot Order Protective Reopening for Different Assessment Year: ITAT Lucknow
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Lucknow ruled that the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] cannot order a protective reopening of assessments for a different assessment year than the one under appeal. The tribunal held that the appellate powers under Section 251 are limited to the assessment year in question, and any directions to reopen completed assessments for another year are beyond its jurisdiction.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT ruled that appellate powers under Section 251 are confined to assessment year under appeal. Directions to reopen completed assessments for another year were held beyond jurisdiction. The post CIT(A) Cannot Order Protective Reopening for Different Assessment Year: ITAT Lucknow appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Karnataka HC allows these firms to use Ashwagandha leaf in health supplements despite FSSAI ban
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has permitted certain firms to use Ashwagandha leaf in health supplements, despite a ban imposed by the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI). The ruling challenges the regulator's decision, raising questions about the oversight of herbal ingredients in health products. This could have implications for food safety regulations and the use of traditional medicinal ingredients in commercial products.

articles.summary: The Election Commission (EC) has increased security measures for the repolling in Falta, West Bengal, by deploying eight Central Armed Police Force (CAPF) personnel at each polling booth. articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore ruled that exemption under Section 54B of the Income Tax Act cannot be denied if agricultural land is purchased within the statutory two-year period, even if procedural non-compliance with the Capital Gains Deposit Scheme occurs. articles.title: Gujarat HC Upholds Reopening of Assessment Based on Alleged Accommodation Entries
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court upheld the reopening of a tax assessment based on alleged accommodation entries. The court ruled that fresh investigation material received by the Assessing Officer was sufficient to justify reassessment under Sections 147 and 148 of the Income Tax Act. The decision emphasizes that prima facie tangible material is adequate at the reopening stage.
articles.raw_summary: Gujarat HC held that reassessment under Sections 147 and 148 was valid where Assessing Officer received fresh investigation material alleging accommodation entries. Court observed that prima facie tangible material is sufficient at reopening stage. articles.title: Indonesian palm oil and coal stocks fall as Subianto government looks to tighten exports
articles.summary: Indonesia is setting up a state-backed body under Danantara to regulate exports of palm oil and coal. This move has caused stock prices of these commodities to fall and raised concerns about global supply and pricing. The policy shift could affect India, a significant importer of Indonesian palm oil and coal, by influencing availability and costs.
articles.raw_summary: Indonesia plans a state backed body under Danantara to control commodity exports, sending palm oil and coal stocks lower and raising global price and supply concerns

articles.title: Interpretation and Relevance of Aspect Theory In Tax Laws
articles.summary: Courts in India have ruled that different aspects of a single transaction can be taxed separately without violating constitutional provisions. This principle allows separate legislatures to levy taxes on distinct taxable elements of the same activity. The ruling clarifies how tax laws apply to complex transactions, ensuring no overlap or conflict in taxation.
articles.raw_summary: Courts have held that distinct aspects of a single transaction may be taxed separately without creating constitutional conflict. The principle permits different legislatures to levy taxes on separate taxable elements of the same activity. The post Interpretation and Relevance of Aspect Theory In Tax Laws appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Haryana cabinet approves new rules: No petrol, diesel vehicles for cab aggregators in NCR
articles.summary: The Haryana Cabinet has approved new rules requiring cab aggregators operating in the National Capital Region (NCR) to use only electric vehicles (EVs) starting in 2026. The decision also includes mandates for safety and insurance norms, considers full tax exemptions for EVs, and plans to purchase 500 electric buses. This move aims to reduce pollution and promote cleaner transportation in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Haryana Cabinet approves clean fuel only rules for NCR aggregator fleets from 2026, mandates safety and insurance norms, considers full tax exemption on EVs, to buy 500 electric buses.

articles.title: One-day nationwide chemists' strike begins on mixed note
articles.summary: A nationwide strike by chemists began, led by the All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists (AIOCD). They are protesting two government notifications (GSR 817 and GSR 220), which they argue legitimize online pharmacies. The strike highlights concerns over the regulation of pharmaceutical sales and access to medicines. AIOCD General Secretary Rajiv Singhal reiterated the protest against these notifications.
articles.raw_summary: AIOCD General Secretary Rajiv Singhal reiterated that AIOCD is protesting the GSR 817 and GSR 220 notifications, as these are effectively regularising online pharmacies.

articles.title: Power Ministry launches rewards scheme for officials who cut fuel use, promotes carpooling and WFH
articles.summary: India's Power Ministry has launched a pilot rewards scheme to incentivize government officials who reduce personal vehicle use, promote carpooling, and encourage work-from-home arrangements. The initiative aims to lower fuel consumption and reduce emissions by changing official behavior. The program targets central government employees as part of broader energy conservation efforts.

articles.title: RBI cancels licence of Yashwant Co-operative Bank in Maharashtra over weak financial position
articles.summary: The RBI said the Phaltan-based co-operative lender failed to meet regulatory requirements under the Banking Regulation Act and would be unable to repay depositors fully in its current financial condition. The central bank added that 99.02% of depositors are eligible to receive their entire deposits under the DICGC insurance cover.

articles.title: MRPL gets PNGRB nod for ATF pipeline to Bengaluru airport
articles.summary: Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals Ltd (MRPL) has received approval from the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (PNGRB) to set up an aviation turbine fuel (ATF) pipeline to Bengaluru airport. The pipeline will have a capacity of 2.5 million tonnes per annum. This project is part of India's infrastructure development in the energy sector, aimed at improving fuel supply efficiency to airports.
articles.raw_summary: The system capacity in the petroleum and petroleum product pipeline shall be equal to 2.5 million tonnes per annum

articles.title: Noida protest: SC orders UP Police to produce accused in court amid custodial torture allegations
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered the Uttar Pradesh Police to produce in court the accused individuals involved in a Noida protest, following allegations of custodial torture. The counsel for one of the accused's brothers sought an independent investigation into the matter. This order highlights concerns over police conduct and the protection of detainees' rights.
articles.raw_summary: The counsel for the brother of one of the men arrested in the matter sought an independent investigation.

articles.title: Patna HC Dismisses GST Challenge Due to Admission of Interest & Penalty Liability
articles.summary: The Patna High Court dismissed a GST challenge after a taxpayer's representative admitted liability for interest and penalties during a personal hearing. The court ruled that the petitioner could not later contradict this stance in writ proceedings. The decision reinforces the legal principle that admissions made during proceedings bind the parties involved.
articles.raw_summary: Patna HC refused to interfere with GST proceedings after taxpayer’s representative admitted liability towards interest and penalty during personal hearing. articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that once a court has decided a threshold objection and the decision becomes final, the same issue cannot be re-agitated in later proceedings. This decision impacts how repetitive applications under Order VII Rule 11 of the Civil Procedure Code (CPC) are handled in courts. The ruling clarifies procedural limits to prevent misuse of legal recourse.

articles.title: SC to Decide Whether Dividend Distribution Tax Is Tax on Dividend or Distributed Profits
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has framed key questions on whether the Dividend Distribution Tax (DDT) under Section 115-O is a tax on shareholder dividend income or on company profits. The ruling will decide if foreign shareholders can benefit from lower tax rates under Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements (DTAA). The case involves a legal dispute over the interpretation of tax laws and their implications for corporate taxation in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has framed substantial questions on whether DDT under Section 115-O is a tax on shareholder dividend income or on company profits. The ruling may determine applicability of DTAA treaty rates on dividend distributions to foreign shareholders. The post SC to Decide Whether Dividend Distribution Tax Is Tax on Dividend or Distributed Profits appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ‘Bail is the rule even in UAPA cases’: SC says its verdict on Khalid, Imam pleas ignored precedent

The court criticized the lower court's order, noting that its earlier verdict on similar pleas was ignored. This ruling highlights the judiciary's stance on bail provisions in stringent anti-terror laws, impacting fundamental rights and detention policies.

articles.title: Bail Prevails Over UAPA Bar in Long Incarceration Cases: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that constitutional courts can grant bail in cases under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA) when prolonged incarceration and delayed trials violate Article 21 of the Constitution. The Court clarified that statutory restrictions under Section 43D(5) of the UAPA cannot justify indefinite pre-trial detention. This decision addresses concerns about prolonged detention without trial, emphasizing the protection of fundamental rights.

articles.title: Not activating countercyclical capital buffer, says RBI
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has decided not to activate the countercyclical capital buffer. This measure is not needed at this time. The buffer is designed to build capital in good times for use during difficult periods. It also aims to curb excessive lending. The framework was introduced after the 2008 global financial crisis.

The ruling was reported by TaxGuru.

articles.title: RBI discontinues investment fluctuation reserve requirement for banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has discontinued the Investment Fluctuation Reserve for commercial banks. This change takes effect from May 18, 2026. Balances in the IFR will be transferred to other reserves. The Reserve Bank has issued separate circulars for cooperative banks, small finance banks, and payments banks. This move aligns with updated prudential frameworks for market risk and investments.

This move aligns with updated prudential frameworks for market risk and investments.

articles.title: DRP 180526: Ethanol Production in Drought prone areas: A false solution?
articles.summary: From a tonne of sugarcane juice (about 1,000 litres), only 70 litres of ethanol is produced. The process is doubly water-intensive: first, to grow a crop that guzzles vast quantities of water, and then, to expend further energy and resources to extract ethanol from it. With govt policy incentives, India’s ethanol production capacity has increased dramatically — from about 518 crore litres a decade ago to nearly 2,000 crore litres today. However, current demand is only about 1,100 crore litres. I

articles.title: In Himachal Pradesh, tourism and migration are altering beliefs that kept forests safe
articles.summary: Traditional sacred groves in Himachal Pradesh, protected by local beliefs, are facing threats from tourism and migration. These forest patches, preserved for generations, are losing their cultural significance, raising concerns about long-term environmental conservation. Major economies have also seen their growth outlooks downgraded.

articles.title: What NRIs should know about gift received from grandparents
articles.summary: The article clarifies whether gifts received from maternal or paternal grandparents are covered under the Income Tax Act, 1961. Judicial precedents suggest that the term includes both maternal and paternal lineal ascendants and descendants. This interpretation is relevant for Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) navigating tax laws related to gifts.
articles.raw_summary: While ITA 1961 did not expressly clarify whether the term covered maternal as well as paternal relatives, there are judicial precedents supporting the view that the expression includes both maternal and paternal lineal ascendants and descendants.

articles.title: From Intelligence To Empathy: Reimagining Role Of Emotional Intelligence (EQ) In Legal Profession
articles.summary: The article argues that the legal profession in India, traditionally focused on intellect and logic, needs to incorporate Emotional Intelligence (EQ) to address real-world challenges. It suggests that the emphasis on Intelligence Quotient (IQ) alone is insufficient, and a balance with EQ could improve legal practice. The piece is an opinion piece from LiveLaw, discussing the evolving role of EQ in legal education and practice.

From classrooms to courtrooms the emphasis has traditionally been on the relevance of Intelligence Quotient (IQ) i.e. the ability to analyse, argue and adjudicate. However, the lived realities within the legal profession increasingly reveal a silent yet an urgent need for Emotional Intelligence (EQ). articles.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a global health emergency
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared a global health emergency due to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. Over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths have been reported, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. The outbreak raises concerns for neighboring countries, including India, due to potential cross-border spread and the need for coordinated international health responses.

The directive follows the state's stance that bike taxis operate outside legal frameworks.

articles.title: Can TVK’s Farm Promises Change Tamil Nadu Agriculture?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's farm politics is heating up ahead of the 2026 Assembly elections. The ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) government has focused on irrigation, subsidies, and welfare through a separate agriculture budget. In contrast, the opposition party TVK (led by Vijay) is promising direct economic relief to farmers, including crop loan waivers, higher minimum support prices (MSPs), and support for tenant farmers. These promises could shape future agricultural policies if implemented.
articles.raw_summary: Tamil Nadu’s farm politics is entering a new phase ahead of the 2026 Assembly elections. While the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam government focused on irrigation, subsidies, mechanization and welfare through its separate agriculture budget, Vijay’s TVK is promising direct economic relief through crop loan waivers, higher MSPs and support for tenant farmers.

articles.title: EXIM Bank eyes 10% jump in loan book in FY27 despite West Asia tensions
articles.summary: Export-Import Bank of India expects its loan book to grow 10% in FY27 despite geopolitical tensions in West Asia, according to Managing Director and CEO Harsha Bangari. articles.title: Amazon’s third-party logistics biz does not make any sense: Delhivery CEO
articles.summary: Delhivery CEO Sahil Barua criticized Amazon's third-party logistics business model, arguing that captive networks prioritize Amazon's own orders, making the service less viable and costlier for external clients. Barua's comments highlight concerns about the sustainability of Amazon's logistics expansion in India's competitive delivery market.

The upgrades could influence India's defence strategy and procurement decisions in the future.

articles.summary: Karnataka launched its first agriculture and allied innovation center of excellence in Kalaburagi, managed by the Krishi Kalpa Foundation. This initiative aims to modernize agriculture in the region through innovation and technology adoption.

articles.title: India’s rising oil imports push trade deficit into risky territory: Crisil
articles.summary: India’s oil trade deficit is expected to widen significantly in FY27 due to rising crude oil prices and weakening petroleum exports, according to a report by Crisil. The report warned that India’s heavy dependence on imported crude could put added pressure on the country’s external finances, with the current account deficit also projected to rise as oil-related trade pressures intensify.

articles.title: West Bengal rice industry seeks infra push, faster clearances from new BJP govt
articles.summary: West Bengal's rice sector urges the new state government for better infrastructure and quicker approvals. Industry leaders highlight the need for improved rural roads, electricity, and a single-window clearance system. They also advocate for a dedicated Rice Industry Development Board. Enhanced rail and port connectivity is crucial for boosting exports of premium rice varieties like Gobindo Bhog and Tulai Panji.

articles.title: Iran war to slow down India’s FY27 economic growth: rating agencies
articles.summary: Two rating agencies have cut their FY27 GDP forecasts as the West Asia war drives crude oil toward $95–110 a barrel, stokes inflation, and forces fuel price hikes. The government's own target of 7–7.4% growth, set before the war began, now looks out of reach.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked again! Fuel prices increased by 90 paise per litre in 2nd hike in a week
articles.summary: Petrol and diesel prices in India were increased by 90 paise per litre on Tuesday, marking the second price hike in less than a week. The increase affects consumers and businesses reliant on fuel. Fuel prices in India are influenced by central and state taxes, global crude oil prices, and government policies. The article does not cite a direct government or regulatory action but reflects a trend that may prompt policy discussions on fuel pricing mechanisms.
articles.raw_summary: Petrol and diesel prices were increased by 90 paise per litre on Tuesday, the second increase in less than a week.

articles.title: Oil prices up over 2% after drone attack on UAE's nuclear power plant
articles.summary: Oil prices surged over 2% following a drone strike near the Barakah nuclear power plant in the UAE. The attack heightened tensions in West Asia and raised concerns about oil supply disruptions. The U.S. issued warnings to Iran, further escalating the situation.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices rose over 2% after a drone strike near the Barakah nuclear plant in the UAE, as renewed West Asia tensions and US warnings to Iran stoked supply fears.

articles.title: Fuel supply delays hit BPCL, HPCL outlets amid panic buying fears
articles.summary: Fuel supplies from Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have been delayed by two to three days in some cases over the past three weeks. Several outlets are receiving only about 80% of their usual orders, raising concerns about potential panic buying. BPCL and HPCL are government-owned oil marketing companies.

articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) increased petrol and diesel prices by about ₹3 per litre, ending a two-year price freeze. The move follows rising crude oil prices, which have led to financial losses for the firms. The price hike could impact inflation and consumer spending, potentially prompting government or regulatory responses.
articles.raw_summary: After over two years of holding prices steady, state-run oil firms raised petrol and diesel prices by about ₹3 a litre, citing mounting under-recoveries amid elevated crude oil prices.

The stability comes despite supply tightness caused by the West Asia conflict and the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with the International Energy Agency warning of a growing global oil shortfall. The outcome of the Trump-Xi meeting could impact global oil markets and trade dynamics, which are critical for India's energy and economic policies.

articles.title: CBI searches 7 locations in Haryana IDFC First Bank–AU Small Finance Bank fraud case
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) conducted raids at seven locations in Chandigarh and Panchkula, Haryana, as part of an investigation into a banking fraud case involving IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank. So far, 16 individuals have been arrested. The case raises concerns about regulatory oversight and financial governance in India's banking sector.
articles.raw_summary: The CBI raided seven locations in Chandigarh and Panchkula in the Haryana bank scam linked to IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank, with 16 accused arrested so far.

articles.title: India considers PLI scheme for seafood sector to increase value-added exports
articles.summary: The Fisheries Department will explore the possibility of formulating a productionlinked incentive (PLI) scheme for MSMEs in the seafood sector, an official statement said on Thursday.
articles.raw_summary: The Fisheries Department will explore the possibility of formulating a productionlinked incentive (PLI) scheme for MSMEs in the seafood sector, an official statement said on Thursday.

articles.title: WHO warns as Bundibugyo Ebola spreads from Congo to Uganda amid resource gaps
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised concerns about a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, with 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases reported. The virus has now spread to Uganda, raising fears of further regional spread. Health workers are struggling to contain the outbreak due to resource gaps. This situation may prompt India to review its public health preparedness and border control measures.
articles.raw_summary: WHO has raised alarm over a fast-growing Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, with 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases reported as health workers struggle to contain the virus.

articles.title: Southwest monsoon reaches Andaman and Nicobar region ahead of Kerala onset: IMD
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported that the southwest monsoon has reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands ahead of its expected onset in Kerala on May 26. The monsoon's advance over the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and the Andaman region is seen as favorable for further progress in the coming days. This development is significant for India's agricultural planning and water resource management, as the monsoon plays a crucial role in the country's farming cycles and overall economy.
articles.raw_summary: The southwest monsoon has advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and the Andaman region, the IMD said, adding that conditions remain favourable for further progress over the next few days ahead of the expected Kerala onset on May 26.

articles.title: West Bengal | Who is Damayanti Sen, the cop who came in from the cold?
articles.summary: Damayanti Sen became widely known during the 2012 Park Street rape case in Kolkata. Read all about the IPS officer who successfully investigated the rape case that then CM Mamata Banerjee had summarily dismissed as 'fabricated,' and was subsequently shunted around in multiple low-key roles in Kolkata Police. CM Suvendu Adhikari has now appointed her to helm a panel on crimes against women that were committed in the TMC era.

articles.title: Odisha says fuel shortage caused by panic buying, assures normal supply in 48 hours
articles.summary: The Odisha government attributed fuel shortages across the state to panic buying and temporary supply chain disruptions. Gig workers, fishermen, and transport operators reported severe impacts on their livelihoods. The government assured normal fuel supply within 48 hours.
articles.raw_summary: The Odisha government has blamed panic buying and temporary supply chain disruptions for fuel shortages reported across the state, even as gig workers, fishermen and transport operators say the crisis has severely affected livelihoods.

articles.title: See long-term opportunity in auto parts, aerospace and chemicals: AB Sun Life AMC’s Harish Krishnan
articles.summary: Harish Krishnan of Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC said the rupee’s sharp fall may be nearing its final phase as the government and RBI step up stabilisation measures. He highlighted engineering exports, chemicals, aerospace and IT services as long-term investment themes, while cautioning that margin pressure from rising input costs could continue over the next two quarters.

articles.title: Puducherry: AIADMK MLA Anbalagan to be sworn-in as pro tem Speaker on Monday
articles.summary: Anbalagan, the Secretary of the Puducherry unit of AIADMK, will be sworn in as the pro tem Speaker on Monday. This appointment is part of the legislative process in Puducherry. articles.title: Maneka Gandhi says Supreme Court 'has done nothing', stray dogs order draws criticism from activists
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld restrictions on stray dogs near public places and emphasized enforcement of Animal Birth Control (ABC) rules. Maneka Gandhi criticized the order, calling the Supreme Court's action ineffective. Animal rights activists also opposed the ruling. The decision has significant implications for urban governance and animal welfare policies in India.

articles.summary: The son of Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar has been arrested in connection with a POCSO case involving a 17-year-old alleged victim. Statements from the victim and her mother were recorded before a magistrate as part of the investigation. The case highlights concerns about child safety and legal protections under India's POCSO Act.

The government's fiscal situation is not directly affected by this price adjustment. State governments may see revenue changes if excise duties are altered.

articles.title: It’s a summer of discontent for delivery riders as incentives dip
articles.summary: Delivery riders in India held a nationwide strike on Saturday to protest declining earnings amid rising fuel prices. Companies have limited pay hikes to high-demand areas instead of broad increases. The riders demand higher payouts and a revised incentive structure to address their financial pressures.

articles.title: ICRA sees Q4 growth slowing to three-qtr low of 7%
articles.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026. ICRA forecasts a 7% growth rate, down from the previous quarter. This slowdown is attributed to weaker industrial and services sector expansion. Agriculture is expected to see a slight improvement. ICRA also anticipates a full fiscal year 2026 GDP growth of 7.5%.

articles.title: Ind-Ra says West Asia tensions, El Nino to drag FY27 growth to 6.7%
articles.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.7% in 2026-27. Higher crude oil and food prices, along with West Asia conflict uncertainty, will impact growth. A potential El Nino event could also affect agriculture. India Ratings and Research (Ind-Ra) made this projection. Inflation is expected to be 4.4% in 2026-27.

Bilateral trade is growing strongly, with a target of $200 billion by 2032.

articles.title: India's inflation puzzle: How will fuel price play out
articles.summary: India's inflation debate intensifies as wholesale prices surge, driven by fuel costs, while retail inflation remains subdued. A recent fuel price hike is expected to push retail inflation higher, impacting household budgets. The RBI will closely monitor global crude prices, monsoon impact, and the pass-through of wholesale to retail inflation before adjusting interest rates.

articles.title: Downside risks to economy have begun materializing as continued West Asia conflict casts shadow over India's trade outlook: Crisil
articles.summary: The escalating conflict in West Asia is emerging as a major risk for India’s economy, threatening trade flows, increasing import costs, and putting pressure on growth. Higher crude oil and transportation expenses are expected to widen India’s current account deficit and push inflation upward as businesses pass on rising costs to consumers. Economists warn that prolonged geopolitical tensions could slow GDP growth and strain the country’s external finances.

Economists warn that prolonged geopolitical tensions could slow GDP growth and strain the country’s external finances.

articles.title: India soymeal exports to fall to four-year low on rising prices
articles.summary: India's soymeal exports are projected to drop by half to a four-year low in the current year, driven by a 47% price surge over the past month. This makes Indian animal feed uncompetitive against South American supplies, prompting Asian buyers to switch. Tight domestic supplies and strong demand are keeping prices elevated.

articles.title: El Nino spells trouble for kharif harvests across India
articles.summary: A study by scientists at an ICAR institute has found that El Niño events have consistently reduced yields of major kharif crops in India, with significant impacts across multiple states. Research led by Subash N Pillai at the ICAR-Indian Institute of Farming Systems Research shows that paddy output fell by over 10% in 77 districts, while maize production declined similarly in 65 districts during El Niño years.

articles.summary: Animal rights activist Maneka Gandhi said India can sharply reduce stray dog attacks and population levels within two years through proper implementation of district-level sterilisation centres, while advocate Nanita Sharma questioned authorities’ commitment to enforcing long-pending animal birth control rules after the Supreme Court’s latest directions.

articles.title: Gold to revisit record highs, Brent’s new floor seen at $85-95: Ole Hansen
articles.summary: Saxo Bank’s Ole Hansen predicts crude oil markets will remain tight even if geopolitical tensions ease, while gold prices could reach new highs due to rising fiscal debt concerns, de-dollarisation trends, and persistent inflation. The analysis highlights structural supply constraints in oil and macroeconomic factors driving gold prices, which have direct implications for India’s energy imports and inflation management.

articles.title: Former Uttarakhand CM B C Khanduri dies at 91 after prolonged illness

articles.summary: Food safety officials in Meerut seized nearly 70 tins of refined oil and 8 sacks of skimmed milk powder during a raid. The operation targeted contaminated khoya (a dairy product) weighing 800 kg, which was destroyed. Food adulteration poses health risks and may prompt regulatory bodies like FSSAI to strengthen enforcement or introduce stricter measures for dairy and edible oil sectors.

articles.title: China’s economy slows sharply as investment resumes declines
articles.summary: China's economy slowed sharply in April, with fixed asset investment declining by 1.6%, retail sales growing by just 0.2%, and industrial output rising by 4.1%. articles.title: Powerful in-laws, missing husband, CCTV twist: 5 details fuelling suspicion in Bhopal dowry case
articles.summary: A dowry death case in Bhopal has raised suspicions due to the accused family's influence, alleged lapses in the investigation, and CCTV footage from the night of the incident. The case has sparked outrage over gender-based violence and systemic failures in addressing such crimes. The incident may prompt calls for stricter enforcement of anti-dowry laws and judicial reforms.
articles.raw_summary: The Bhopal dowry death case has sparked outrage over the accused family’s influence, alleged probe lapses and CCTV footage from the night of the incident.

articles.title: Manipur hostage crisis: Shutdowns, blockade continue to disrupt normal lives
articles.summary: Two church teams visited Manipur's Kangpokpi and Senapati districts to mediate between the Kuki and Naga communities amid ongoing hostage crises and shutdowns. The conflict has disrupted daily life in the region. The teams aim to broker peace between the two communities. The situation reflects underlying tensions that could require government intervention.
articles.raw_summary: Two church teams visited Kangpokpi and Senapati districts to try and broker peace between the Kuki and Naga communities

articles.title: School breaks silence after intense backlash over asking students if parents' jobs are ‘noisy, smelly’
articles.summary: A school faced intense backlash after a survey asked students whether their parents' jobs were 'noisy, smelly, or dirty.' The school defended the survey, but reactions on social media were mixed. The incident raises questions about inclusivity and sensitivity in educational practices, potentially prompting discussions on school policies or civil liberties concerns.
articles.raw_summary: The school’s explanation of their survey asking students about their parents' jobs has received mixed reactions on social media.

articles.title: Global lessons from Ebola emergency
articles.summary: The article compares Ebola transmission (symptomatic) with COVID-19 and notes that pandemic preparedness frameworks have weakened since the Ebola emergency six years ago. It underscores the need for robust health policies to handle future outbreaks, though it does not mention India specifically.
articles.raw_summary: Ebola transmits when patients are symptomatic, unlike the coronavirus of 2020. But six years later, pandemic preparedness frameworks have weakened

articles.title: Explainer-Why India's Tata Sons is facing pressure to list
articles.summary: Until now, Tata Sons has remained unlisted. But there is now pressure to list from internal stakeholders, including its second largest shareholder, the Shapoorji Paloonji (SP) Group. Rules from the Reserve Bank of India may also require them to list unless they can secure an exemption.

Rules from the Reserve Bank of India may also require them to list unless they can secure an exemption.
